header.title: Simple Bottom Sheet
header.description: A commonly used panel. Resizable, customizable according to its use, very versatile for everything.

basic.title: Basic Usage (Auto-adjusting)
basic.description: The simplest implementation. The panel expands vertically to fit its internal content.

height.title: With Fixed Height and Backdrop
height.description: Using the {height} prop establishes a strict height. Whether the content is smaller or larger, the panel will respect that limit. Enabling {backdrop} adds the dark background layer. Additionally, you can add the {closeOnBackdrop} property if you want the panel to close when making any gesture on the backdrop.

custom.title: Border and Color Customization (Local)
custom.description: Components are highly customizable, you can overwrite CSS variables by passing a class to the component. The variables will locally affect this panel without touching others. Note the red border and rounded corners.
custom.dark_mode: This same technique can be used to apply {dark_mode}. Adding a class with different variables or directly changing the global CSS variables.

persistent.title: Persistent Panel
persistent.description: A panel that does not close with any gesture, ideal for information that strictly requires the user. To close the panel, it is necessary to close via an internal button by modifying the {vmodel}.

header.title: Custom Header via Slots
header.description: If the native header is not enough, you can inject your own design. This allows adding buttons, icons, or complex layouts.

zindex.title: Stacking with zIndex
zindex.description: The {zIndex} prop controls the stacking order when multiple panels are open simultaneously. The effective value is {base}. Each panel can be opened from within another, and the one with the highest {zIndex} will always stay at the front; ideal for step-by-step wizards or cascading modal confirmations.

props.model: Controls the visibility of the fixed panel.
props.title: Basic title printed in the native header.
props.height: Forced height of the panel. If omitted, the panel automatically adjusts to the content height.
props.backdrop: Shows a semi-transparent dark overlay behind the panel.
props.props_obj: Additional object to bind extra payloads to the component if necessary.

events.model: Bidirectional synchronization of the visibility state.
events.opened: Triggered when the panel has finished appearing on screen.
events.closed: Triggered when the panel has finished hiding and is removed from the DOM.
events.before_close: Emitted just before starting the closing animation.

slots.default: Renders the full body of the panel without header control.
slots.header: Completely replaces the native header area, allowing for fully custom layouts.

header.title: Dynamic Bottom Sheet
header.description: A dynamic bottom panel that allows a dynamic interaction with the content.

callout.desc: this panel can be resized into three different states. {collapsed}, {half} and {full}. Very useful for dynamic actions that require an interaction without losing page context.

simple.title: Simple Usage
simple.description: A basic bottom sheet with only content and default close behavior. Uses the configured height.

backdrop.title: Usage with Backdrop
backdrop.description: Enables a dark layer in the background by adding the {prop} property. Additionally, clicking on the backdrop collapses the container.

slots.title: Slots (Header and Collapsed)
slots.desc1: The {collapsed} slot shows custom content when the container is in the {state_collapsed} state.
slots.desc2: The {header} slot shows custom content in the container's header.

dark_mode.title: Integrated Dark Mode Usage
dark_mode.description: Injecting variables to apply the respective local dark theme directly to a bottom sheet.

custom_handle.title: Handle Customization
custom_handle.description: You can override CSS variables by passing a class to the component. The variables will affect this sheet locally.
custom_handle.note: Note: It is recommended to apply styles globally or in a component without using {scoped}.

zindex.title: Stacking with zIndex
zindex.description: The {zIndex} prop allows managing visual order when multiple panels are open at the same time. The effective final value is {base}, ensuring sheets are always above normal app content. Assign increasing values to define which panel stays in front.

props.model: Controls the visibility of the dynamic modal.
props.title: Basic title printed in the native header.
props.full: Height dimension for the maximum 'full' state.
props.showCloseButton: Show or hide the cross close button.
props.showBackdrop: Draws and shows a semi-transparent dark overlay.

events.model: Changes component state and bidirectional sync.
events.close: Triggered at the beginning of the close request.
events.opened: Triggered when the CSS visual entrance animation ends.
events.closed: Triggered when the CSS visual exit animation ends.
events.size_change: Notifies when the size change has finished. The emitted values are of DynamicSize: 'collapsed' | 'half' | 'full'.

slots.header: Slot for the bottomsheet header. If this slot is used, the default header is lost.
slots.collapsed_content: Slot for the bottomsheet content in the 'collapsed' state.

zindex_example.desc1_base: This panel has {code}, which internally adds 9000. It is the first level of the stack.
zindex_example.desc2_base: Now open a second panel on top. Although both share the same mount point ({code}), the one with higher {zIndex} is shown in front.
zindex_example.desc1_top: This panel uses {code} (effective 9002) and appears over the previous panel.
zindex_example.desc2_top: Close it to return to the base panel. Stacking is fully controlled by the {zIndex} prop.

hero.title: Bottom Sheet Wrappers
hero.subtitle: A flexible Vue 3 library specifically designed for {mobile} with native touch gesture support and distinct display modes.

simple.title: Simple
simple.description: A {panel} or one that adjusts to its content size. Simple to use for any type of component.

dynamic.title: Dynamic
dynamic.description: A {panel}, ideal for content that requires fluid touch interaction.

register.title: 2. Registration and Import
register.description: {important} step for the component to look and act correctly in your project. You only need to import the stylesheet and the components you want to use. You can import and use components directly in your {ext} file:
register.alternative: Or you can import the stylesheet in {file} to make it available globally:

usage.title: 3. Component Usage
usage.description: Now you can use the component in your project. In this example, simple usage for {mode} mode

customization.title: Customization
customization.description: The library uses a {vars} system with the {prefix} prefix designed to be flexible and easy to theme through inheritance.

base.title: Main (Base)
base.description: They control multiple elements at once:

customization.footer: You can apply these variables globally in your {root} or specifically in a container to create custom themes quickly:
